Thank you so much for your time just now, and it was nice talking to you.

As requested, please complete and sign the attached tax representative appointment letter and return to us a scanned copy. We will then submit it to the Inland Revenue Department. As mentioned, this letter authorizes Deloitte to handle your HK salaries tax affairs on your behalf, and we will also be able to collect a copy of your previous HK tax filings.

Sorry for not getting back to you earlier.

We hereby enclose the updated checklist for your records. The same password applies.

We understand that you should have filed your 2022/23 HK Individual Tax Return via eTAX. Please note that we could collect a copy of your filed tax return from the Inland Revenue Department once we are appointed as your tax representative. As such, we would be grateful if you can kindly provide / advise the following for our further handling of your HK salaries tax matters.

We understand that you should have filed your 2022/23 HK Individual Tax Return via eTAX. Please note that we can collect a copy of your filed tax return from the Inland Revenue Department once we are appointed as your tax representative. As such, we would appreciate it if you could kindly provide/advice the following information for us to further handle your HK salaries tax matters.

-	International transfer from Hong Kong to Singapore.
-	Winata is single and no dependent in HK. No property income and no business income in HK. No equity-based compensation.
-	HK employment end date is June 30, 2023, and Singapore employment start date will be July 1, 2023.
-	He will be leaving HK tomorrow (i.e. June 29, 2023).
-	Per Winata, he has received the Form IR56G from HK HR yesterday.
-	He started his HK employment since June 1, 2021 and Vialto handled his 2021/22 ITR. 

please check

- International transfer from Hong Kong to Singapore.
- Winata is single and has no dependents in HK. There is no property income or business income in HK, and no equity-based compensation.
- HK employment end date is June 30, 2023, and Singapore employment start date will be July 1, 2023.
- Winata will be leaving HK tomorrow (i.e., June 29, 2023).
- According to Winata, he received the Form IR56G from HK HR yesterday.
- He started his HK employment on June 1, 2021, and Vialto handled his 2021/22 Individual Tax Return (ITR).

-	Advised him that DTTHK is authorized for his departure tax briefing and the HK ITR for the year of transfer (i.e. 2023/24).
-	In that case, he will be handling the 2022/23 ITR himself. He acknowledged and confirmed that he has yet to file the 2022/23 ITR as of today. 
-	He asked if DTTHK can also assist in his 2022/23 tax filings. Explained to him that we currently are authorized for 2023/24 only. He said he will check with JLL himself and request the authorization for 2022/23. He will let us know later. 
-	SG team has yet to contact him, but he probably not requires for SG tax assistance (and as such, he will negotiate with the company for HK 2022/23 tax assistance). 

check please

- I advised him that DTTHK is authorized for his departure tax briefing and the HK Individual Tax Return (ITR) for the year of transfer (i.e., 2023/24).
- In that case, he will be handling the 2022/23 ITR himself. He acknowledged and confirmed that he has yet to file the 2022/23 ITR as of today.
- He asked if DTTHK can also assist in his 2022/23 tax filings. I explained to him that we are currently authorized for 2023/24 only. He said he will check with JLL himself and request the authorization for 2022/23. He will let us know later.
- The SG team has yet to contact him, but he probably does not require SG tax assistance (and as such, he will negotiate with the company for HK 2022/23 tax assistance).

Please note that some details may require additional context, as the provided sentences are concise.

•	Your start date with JLL Singapore will be July 1, 2023. Your JLL HK employment end date is June 30, 2023 and you are leaving Hong Kong on June 29, 2023.

•	You are single with no dependent in Hong Kong. Your have no property income nor business income in Hong Kong.

•	You are single with no dependents in Hong Kong. You have no property income or business income in Hong Kong.

•	Please be reminded that if a person visits HK for more than 60 days in an assessment year (from April 1 to March 31 of the following year) may result in salaries tax being assessed on employment income derived from services rendered in HK.

•	Please note that if a person stays in Hong Kong for more than 60 days during an assessment year (from April 1 to March 31 of the following year), it may lead to the assessment of salaries tax on employment income earned from services rendered in Hong Kong.

Hope you are doing well.

I hope this message finds you well.

As we have not received any further communication from you in response to our previous emails, we would like to follow up again. Could you please provide/advise the following information as soon as possible for us to proceed with your HK tax filings:

- Any updates regarding your HK employment end date and departure from Hong Kong.
- Confirmation if you have filed your 2022/23 HK Individual Tax Return (ITR).
- Authorization for DTTHK to handle your 2022/23 tax filings, if applicable.
- Any updates on your Singapore tax assistance requirements.

Thank you, and we look forward to your prompt response.

I IM Jane that we have replied on Tuesday, June 27. She confirmed receipt.
